meaning: adj.同类的,相似的,均一的,均匀的
examples: Audiences are smaller and more homogeneous than they have been in the past.
The government's avowed aim, however, is to maintain "a substantially homogeneous society into which newcomers, from whatever sources, will merge themselves".
In these days the pre-industrial city in America functioned as a complex and varied organizing element in American life, not as a simple, homogeneous, and static unit.
South Korea may be ethnically homogeneous, but turf politics has historically divided the electorate along geographical lines.
And he will lead a flock of 2.6m that gets less homogeneous by the day.
